fields.definition_origin: Cross-device resume hydration — the ability to begin onboarding on one device, abandon, and resume on another (typically after reinstall or after switching from phone to tablet) — emerged as a named pattern in the 2022-2024 high-CAC mobile-app discourse. The PDF 'Strategic Architecture and Psychological Frameworks for Mobile Application Onboarding' explicitly documents the practice. Petprog's dev.to piece 'Designing Fault-Tolerant Onboarding: How to Resume User Progress After Reinstall' (2024, dev.to/petprog) is a canonical practitioner walkthrough. The pattern has roots in iCloud's NSUbiquitousKeyValueStore (iOS 5, 2011), Google Account state sync (since 2012), and Spotify's account-restored library/playlists (since launch). For onboarding specifically, the practice is newer because pre-2022 wisdom assumed onboarding was a one-shot client flow — the high CAC of 2024-2026 (TikTok ad CPMs >$10) made losing mid-onboarding users economically intolerable.
fields.core_mechanism: When a user uninstalls (frustration, low-storage, accidental) or switches devices mid-onboarding, the canonical user-id (Apple/Google/email/phone) bridges the gap: server-stored onboarding state is keyed by user_id, and on auth-on-new-device, the client requests `/api/onboarding/state` and hydrates the local FSM from server-stored snapshot. The user lands on the exact step they last completed without re-answering questions. Three load-bearing components: (1) PRE-AUTH IDENTITY — pre-account-creation onboarding answers must be associable with the user once they auth (anonymous_id → user_id merge on sign-in). (2) SERVER PERSISTENCE — every transition pushes to server (item 32); server is canonical record. (3) HYDRATION ON AUTH — on sign-in (any device), client fetches server state and merges with any local state (last-write-wins or merge-by-timestamp). The behavioral effect: high-intent users who would otherwise count as churn re-enter the funnel; for high-CAC apps (Cal AI's TikTok ad spend, etc.), recovering 5-15% of these is meaningful. Combined with deep-link entry (item 42 UGC creative alignment), users tapping a re-targeting ad days later land directly at their previous step.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: N/A (architectural). Indirect: avoids extinction of the dopaminergic anticipation built up in prior session — re-asking already-answered questions kills the variable-reward anticipation, making the eventual reveal feel less earned.
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Petprog 2024 dev.to case study: 11% week-1 abandoner recovery via cross-device resume in a small fitness app. (2) Adapty 2026 onboarding analytics: 8-12% of mid-flow abandoners return within 7 days (window where resume captures most value). (3) Supabase + RLS pattern documented in Supabase docs as canonical for user-scoped data with server source of truth. (4) Mobbin/RevenueCat anecdotes: top-grossing apps (Cal AI, Headspace, Headway) all preserve state across reinstall when sign-in is the same. (5) Apple's iCloud Keychain + iCloud Key-Value Store provide infrastructure for cross-device on iOS-only flows (Apple Developer documentation 2011-2026). (6) Disney $10M COPPA settlement Dec 2025 — relevance: cross-device tracking implies persistent identifier collection, regulatory scrutiny on minors. (7) Specific RCTs absent; evidence is practitioner case-study + benchmark inference.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: % of cross-device resumes successful (target 99%+), recovered-user count per week (abandoners who resume +N days later), funnel step on resume (proxy for value of recovered users), session continuity rate, post-resume completion rate (do they finish what they started?).
fields.evidence_strength: case_study + practitioner_opinion (foundational tech is mature; specific onboarding application is recent industry pattern)
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Petprog 2024: +11% recovered abandoners (single-app case). Inferred from Adapty 2026 abandoner-return data: 5-15% of mid-flow abandoners re-engage; cross-device resume captures meaningful fraction. Adapty case studies cite cross-device resume as part of top-decile architectures driving paywall reach >70%.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) ANONYMOUS ID: generate UUIDv7 (or Apple/Google ad-id with consent) on first launch, persist to MMKV. Use as state key pre-auth. (2) ANONYMOUS-TO-AUTH MERGE: on sign-in (Magic Link, Apple/Google sign-in, email/phone OTP), POST anonymous_id + user_id to /api/onboarding/merge — server moves anonymous state into user-scoped record. (3) HYDRATE ON AUTH: after auth, GET /api/onboarding/state?user_id=X. Returns latest snapshot with timestamp + version. (4) MERGE LOGIC: compare server timestamp vs local MMKV timestamp; if server newer, hydrate FSM with server snapshot; if local newer, push local to server first; if same, no-op. (5) DEEP-LINK ENTRY: re-targeting ad tap → universal link / deep link with `flow_id` + signed token → app opens, validates token, hydrates state for that flow_id. (6) APP-INSTALL DEFERRED DEEP LINK: tap install button on web ad → install app → first launch picks up the deferred deep link (Branch.io, AppsFlyer, Firebase Dynamic Links — Apple deprecated 2025) → routes to correct onboarding state. (7) SCHEMA VERSIONING: server state includes schema version; client supports last 3 versions; old client receives downgraded payload OR prompted to update. (8) CONFLICT UI: if local + server states diverge significantly (different answers to same question), prompt user 'You answered X here, Y on another device — keep which?' (rare). (9) NO-AUTH-NO-RESUME EXCEPTION: if user starts onboarding pre-auth, abandons, reinstalls, and signs up with NEW email — there's no link. Document this as expected behavior; show 'Welcome' for fresh user. (10) ACCOUNT RECOVERY UX: 'Sign in to continue where you left off' affordance on welcome screen for returning users (detect via deferred deep link or device-fingerprint heuristic). (11) PRIVACY: collect minimum identity needed; declare cross-device sync in privacy notice. (12) ENCRYPTION: server state encrypted at rest (Supabase pgcrypto / managed-DB encryption). (13) iCloud Backup: iOS UserDefaults / NSUbiquitousKeyValueStore syncs to iCloud automatically — leverage for iOS-only cross-iOS-device. (14) GDPR Right-to-Erasure: deleting user erases server state; client clears MMKV. (15) TELEMETRY: emit onboarding_resumed_from_server { step, days_since_abandonment, device_change } to measure value.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI: empirically resumes mid-flow when same user signs in on new device — Mobbin captures evidence. (2) Headspace 2024: account-tied state preserves preferences across reinstall + new device. (3) Spotify: cross-device library + playback state since launch. (4) Discord: account-tied DMs / channels survive reinstall. (5) Petprog dev.to 2024: documented implementation in fitness app, recovered 11% abandoners. (6) Notion: workspace state cross-device. (7) Linear: invite + onboarding state cross-device when same user signs in. (8) BlueSky onboarding: invite-code state preserved server-side, resume on new device after auth. (9) Counter-example: many older indie apps (pre-2022) lose state on reinstall — leak users who return ready to convert. (10) Counter-example failure: app that prompts 'Re-do onboarding because we updated it!' on reinstall — wastes recovered intent.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary because reinstall + cross-device (phone↔tablet) is the canonical use case. Web equivalent: sign-in on different browser hydrates from same server endpoint; cookies / localStorage don't cross devices, but auth-on-new-device → server fetch achieves same effect. Mobile-specific: deferred deep link on first launch (deep link captured pre-install, redeemed on first launch via Branch.io / AppsFlyer); iCloud Keychain syncs Apple sign-in identity across iOS devices automatically; Android Smart Lock similar for Google sign-in. Web: simpler — auth + fetch.
fields.category_fit: MANDATORY for: high-CAC apps (Cal AI, Flo, Noom, Headspace, BetterMe, Headway — every recovered user is acquisition cost saved), apps with significant cross-device usage (phone↔tablet for wellness/edtech). STRONGLY recommended for: any subscription app where mid-flow abandonment is meaningful. Skippable for: apps with low CAC and short onboarding (1-3 screens), apps with no auth (utilities). Solo-dev caveat: implement after FSM (item 30) + persistence (item 32) are stable; not a Day-1 priority.
fields.solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week. Server endpoint /api/onboarding/state (Supabase Edge Function, 1 day); anonymous-to-auth merge logic (1 day); hydrate-on-auth client logic (1 day); deep-link entry (1-2 days, especially deferred deep links); telemetry + edge cases (1-2 days).
fields.tooling_options: SERVER: Supabase + Postgres + RLS (free tier, $25/mo paid), Firebase Realtime/Firestore, hand-rolled Postgres + REST. AUTH: Supabase Auth, Auth0, Clerk, Stytch — all support email/Magic Link/Apple/Google. DEEP LINKS: Branch.io (paid, comprehensive), AppsFlyer (paid, attribution-focused), Firebase Dynamic Links (Apple deprecated 2025, sunset Aug 2025), Adjust, native universal links (iOS) / app links (Android — free, but no install-deferred). iCloud / Cloud Backup: iOS NSUbiquitousKeyValueStore (free, iOS-only), Android Auto Backup (default, free). EXPERIMENTATION: PostHog feature flag for cross-device experiment routing. ENCRYPTION: pgcrypto (Postgres), AWS KMS, Supabase managed encryption.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude with the explicit hydration sequence. Prompt fragment 1: 'Implement cross-device onboarding resume. (1) On first launch, generate anonymous_id (UUIDv7), persist to MMKV. (2) Every onboarding transition POSTs to /api/onboarding/state with anonymous_id (pre-auth) or user_id (post-auth) including FSM snapshot + timestamp + schema_version. (3) On auth event (sign-in completed), POST /api/onboarding/merge with anonymous_id + user_id; server merges anonymous record into user record. (4) On any app launch where user is authenticated, GET /api/onboarding/state?user_id=X; if server snapshot timestamp > local MMKV timestamp, hydrate FSM via createActor(machine, { snapshot: server }) and emit toast "Welcome back — picking up at step N". (5) If server timestamp < local, push local to server first. (6) If schemas differ, run migration. FORBID: re-asking the user questions they already answered after reinstall. FORBID: silent state divergence. Output Supabase Edge Function endpoints + client hydration logic + deep-link routing.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Implement deferred deep link: integrate Branch.io OR Universal Links + custom token resolution. On install + first launch, check for pending deep link → if it carries flow_id + signed_token (HMAC-SHA256), validate, hydrate state for that flow_id → route to correct onboarding step. Telemetry: onboarding_resumed_from_deep_link { flow_id, days_since_link_generated }.' Prompt fragment 3: 'Edge case: user signs in with new email after onboarding pre-auth — anonymous_id has no user_id link. Show fresh onboarding (do not block). Edge case: user signs in on iPad, server has phone state from yesterday — hydrate, show welcome-back toast.'
fields.stitch_application: Frame the resume-on-new-device experience. Prompt fragment: 'Mockup: cold launch on a new iPad after the user previously got to step 8 of 12 on iPhone. Splash for 800ms, then a short transition (slide-up + spring), arriving at step 9 with a small ephemeral toast (lower-third position, cream surface #F4EFE6, ink-text #1A1816, 13pt Söhne body) reading "Welcome back — picking up where you left off on iPhone" with a small device-icon glyph; toast auto-dismisses in 3 seconds with fade-out. Progress bar shows 9 of 12 (with endowed +2 head-start visualizing 11/14). The user sees their previously-stitched personalization (item 14) intact.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults to ignoring cross-device entirely — fresh onboarding on every install. AI may persist locally but not server-side. AI doesn't merge anonymous-to-auth state. AI doesn't handle deferred deep links. AI assumes auth comes first (often it doesn't — user answers 5 questions pre-auth, then signs up). Counter-prompt: 'Implement anonymous_id from first launch, BEFORE auth. State persists by anonymous_id pre-auth, by user_id post-auth. On auth event, merge anonymous into user (server-side). On every app launch, if authenticated, fetch server state, compare timestamps, hydrate. Implement Branch.io deferred deep link OR Universal Link + token resolution for ad-driven re-entry. FORBID: re-running onboarding for users with valid server state. FORBID: silent failures — emit onboarding_resume_failed telemetry.' AI also may assume Realtime sync (over-engineered) — counter: 'simple snapshot fetch on auth + every 30 mins suffices; Realtime is overkill for onboarding state.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Re-running full onboarding on reinstall when same user signs in — wastes recovered intent. (2) Lost answers across device because pre-auth state has no merge to user_id. (3) Silent state divergence (local has different answers than server) — confuse user, corrupt personalization. (4) No deep-link re-entry — re-targeting ads land on welcome screen instead of last step. (5) Forced re-auth for every cross-device — adds friction. (6) Cross-device sync without privacy disclosure — GDPR / DFA risk. (7) Assuming Apple/Google sign-in is universal — many users sign up with email, lose cross-device-of-different-platform. (8) Heavy-handed welcome-back toast that interrupts flow — keep ephemeral, dismissable. (9) Out-of-band ANALYTICS-driven device fingerprinting to attempt cross-device without consent — privacy/regulatory risk. (10) Schema versioning ignored — old client receives newer schema, crashes.
fields.regulatory_risk: Medium-High depending on data scope. (a) GDPR Art. 5(1)(b) purpose limitation — cross-device tracking requires explicit declaration. (b) GDPR Art. 6 lawful basis — consent or legitimate interest must be established. (c) CCPA / CPRA — consumer right to know cross-device tracking. (d) COPPA 2026 — under-13 cross-device tracking requires verifiable parental consent (Disney $10M settlement Dec 2025 enforcement signal). (e) EU Digital Fairness Act draft Q4 2026 — addictive cross-device re-engagement pattern (notification + deferred deep link luring back) flagged as potential dark pattern. (f) Apple Privacy Nutrition Label / App Store Privacy Report must declare cross-device sync. (g) Google Play Data Safety form must declare. (h) iOS App Tracking Transparency (ATT) — if cross-device implementation uses ad-id (IDFA), requires opt-in. Use opaque server-side identifier (UUIDv7) not ad-id to minimize ATT scope.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple App Store Guideline 5.1.1 user privacy — disclose cross-device sync. Apple Sign In with Apple required IF other social sign-in offered (Guideline 4.8) — leverages Apple's cross-Apple-device identity automatically. Google Play Data Safety — disclose. Firebase Dynamic Links sunset by Apple Aug 2025 — migrate to Universal Links + Branch.io / AppsFlyer for deferred deep links. Apple deprecated some app-fingerprint mechanisms via PrivacyManifest 2024 — use opt-in deferred deep link instead.
fields.accessibility_implications: Resume toast ('Welcome back — at step N') must be accessibility-announced. Focus restoration on resume: programmatically focus the resumed screen's primary CTA so screen-reader users land oriented. Avoid interrupting screen-reader users with toast during their orientation. Provide 'Start over' explicit option for users who want fresh start (cognitive accessibility — can't always remember what they answered).
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Anonymous-to-auth merge edge cases: user signed up pre-auth, abandoned, then signs up with different email — orphaned anonymous state. (2) Multi-device collision: phone + iPad simultaneously editing state → last-write-wins might lose data; consider CRDT / vector clocks if true multi-device editing is real (most onboarding is single-device-at-a-time). (3) Days-old server state: user resumes 30 days later — content/copy may have changed; show updated copy gracefully. (4) Deleted account: cross-device resume must respect deletion (no zombie state). (5) Privacy boundary: cross-device sync of identity questions ('I am a recovering addict') needs encrypted at-rest + access-logged. (6) Network on cold launch: hydration fetch adds 200-500ms — pre-fetch in parallel with splash. (7) Schema migration on resume: state from 6 months ago might use deprecated questions — drop or migrate gracefully. (8) Apple Sign In + iOS-only — cross-platform users (iPhone + Android tablet) need email/phone-based identity. (9) Universal Links require associated-domain configuration; deferred deep links require third-party SDK. (10) Test plan: simulate (a) reinstall same device + sign in same email, (b) reinstall same device + sign in different email, (c) phone → iPad transition, (d) Android → iOS transition, (e) deep link from re-targeting ad after 14 days.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Cross-device assumption depends on multi-device penetration: high in NA/EU (phone + tablet + laptop common), lower in some emerging markets where phone-only is dominant. JP/KR: multi-device ownership high. CN: WeChat ecosystem provides cross-device identity natively (consider WeChat Login integration for CN market). LATAM: phone-only common; cross-device less load-bearing. RTL: resume toast text mirrors. Privacy regimes vary: GDPR (EU), CCPA (CA), LGPD (BR), PIPL (CN), DPA (UK) — disclose cross-device per locale.
fields.prescribed_action: Implement anonymous_id + server-mirror persistence (item 32) from Day 1, enabling cross-device resume as natural consequence. On auth, merge anonymous state into user state. On any app launch with auth, fetch server state and hydrate FSM if newer than local. Wire deferred deep links (Branch.io or Universal Links + token) for re-targeting ad re-entry. Forbid: re-running onboarding for users with valid server state. Disclose cross-device sync in privacy notice. Telemetry: emit onboarding_resumed_from_server with days_since_abandonment to measure recovered-user value.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Direct — cross-device resume preserves all questionnaire answers, never re-asks. Stitched personalization (item 14) reads identically on resumed device because shared server state.
fields.aha_relevance: Indirect but high-value — recovers users who built sunk-cost investment in prior session, lets them reach AI plan reveal (item 12) without re-doing work. Reduces extinction of variable-reward anticipation.
fields.measurement_horizon: Day_7 (recovered abandoner window) + Day_30 (LTV of recovered users)
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Resume welcome-back microcopy archetype-tunable: Caregiver ('Welcome back — we kept your place'), Magician ('The thread continues'), Sage ('Resuming step N of M'), Hero ('Right where you left off'), Lover ('We've been waiting'), Innocent ('Hi again — let's continue'), Jester ('Hey — knew you'd be back').
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against 'lost-recovered-intent on reinstall' (the canonical funnel leak). Pairs with item 32 (Persistence) — server is canonical record, MMKV is local cache. Pairs with item 30 (FSM) — FSM snapshot is the unit of hydration. Pairs with item 42 (UGC Creative ↔ Onboarding Promise Alignment) — re-targeting ad → deferred deep link → resume at correct step.
uncertain: specific RCT-grade lift figures for cross-device resume (case study only)
